# F023. Regime Detector + LightGBM Stack

## Positioning

This is the current regime-aware alpha baseline: HMM-style state detection plus the LightGBM ranker. It is one of the cleanest examples of an executable hybrid in the repo.

## Reference Basis

- [Bayesian Online Changepoint Detection](../../references/papers/0710.3742_bayesian_online_changepoint_detection.pdf) - change-point framing for the regime detector
- [LightGBM: A Highly Efficient Gradient Boosting Decision Tree](../../references/papers/lightgbm_neurips_2017.pdf) - the alpha model sitting behind the regime gate

## Repo Anchors

### Operational Guardrails

Shared safety and execution rails used by every candidate before a signal can touch capital.
- [src/risk/manager.py](../../../src/risk/manager.py) - pre-trade risk gate and position haircut
- [src/brokers/paper.py](../../../src/brokers/paper.py) - paper-safe broker adapter
- [src/monitoring/health.py](../../../src/monitoring/health.py) - freshness, heartbeat, and kill-switch checks
- [src/backtest/engine.py](../../../src/backtest/engine.py) - deterministic offline backtest runner
- [src/backtest/matching_engine.py](../../../src/backtest/matching_engine.py) - queue-aware synthetic fill model
- [scripts/monitor_health.py](../../../scripts/monitor_health.py) - runtime health loop and stale-data monitor

### Market Data and Features

Point-in-time data loading plus the shared feature builder used by most signal stacks.
- [src/ingestion/loader.py](../../../src/ingestion/loader.py) - point-in-time market data loading
- [src/features/builder.py](../../../src/features/builder.py) - technical, lagged, and derived feature construction

### Regime and State

Regime detection and state overlays used when the signal should shrink or route rather than trade blind.
- [src/models/hmm.py](../../../src/models/hmm.py) - latent regime detector

### Cross-Sectional Ranker

The executable ranking path that turns scores into a tradable long/flat or top-k book.
- [src/models/lgbm.py](../../../src/models/lgbm.py) - LightGBM scoring model
- [src/features/builder.py](../../../src/features/builder.py) - feature matrix construction for the ranker

## Data / Feature Bundle

- Use the shared market loader and feature builder as the alpha input layer.
- Feed the regime detector with price, vol, and any macro features that can be aligned point-in-time.
- Keep the ranker input identical to F001 so the regime effect can be isolated cleanly.

## Execution Path

- Detect the regime first and then allow the ranker to trade only in the permitted states.
- Route the score through the same risk haircut and backtest engine as the other baselines.
- Use the paper broker and health monitor to prove the gate behaves in mock-safe mode.

## Risk Controls

- Do not let the gate flip too often; regime noise is worse than a missing regime.
- Freeze the model if data freshness or heartbeat checks fail.
- Keep the ranker and the regime state on the same clock to avoid hidden leakage.

## Validation Gates

- Check regime-stratified return and drawdown against F001.
- Measure whether the gate reduces losses during stressed periods without killing normal performance.
- Re-run the smoke/backtest path to confirm the gating logic is deterministic.

## Archive Triggers

- Archive if the gate does not improve drawdown or stability.
- Archive if the regime detector becomes noisier than a rolling-vol heuristic.
- Archive if the gating logic makes the score harder to maintain than it is worth.

## Next Step

Use this as the direct executable baseline for regime-aware scoring.
